compiled_state.title: merge-to-main: _cleanup_stale_git_state does not reset staged changes in main repo -> merge fails with 'local changes would be overwritten'
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## Incident Overview
When the main repo has staged (indexed) changes from a prior session that were never committed or reset, merge-to-main.sh fails at _phase_merge with 'Your local changes to the following files would be overwritten by merge'. The _cleanup_stale_git_state function does not detect or clear staged-only changes (it only handles merge/rebase state).

## Steps to Reproduce
1. In a prior session, stage files in the main repo (git add) but don't commit
2. Start a new worktree session and make commits
3. Run merge-to-main.sh — it will fail at the merge phase

## Expected Behavior
_cleanup_stale_git_state should detect staged changes via 'git diff --cached --quiet' and either reset them or warn before proceeding.

## Actual Behavior
The function passes, _phase_merge checks out main, and git merge fails because staged files conflict with incoming changes.

## Fix Direction
Add a staged-changes check to _cleanup_stale_git_state: run 'git diff --cached --quiet'; if non-zero, run 'git reset HEAD --quiet' to unstage (preserving working tree).

comments.body: Classification: behavioral, Score: 1 (BASIC). Known issue partial match: git stash behavior.
comments.author: Test

comments.body: Gate 1a: no CLI_user tag — proceeding with intent-search

comments.body: Gate 1a: intent-aligned (high confidence). Proceeding to BASIC investigation.

comments.body: Fix applied: Added staged-changes check to _cleanup_stale_git_state in merge-to-main.sh. After rebase/merge cleanup, checks git diff --cached --quiet and runs git reset HEAD --quiet if staged changes exist.
comments.author: Test
